name: Cream Horns
recipeInstructions: Roll the pastry into a 15-inch by 9-inch rectangle. Cut into 9 strips, each 15 inches long and 1 inch wide. Starting at one end of the mold, wrap one strip around an ungreased 4-inch metal cream horn mold, winding it spiral-fashion and overlapping edges about 1/4 inch. Place on a lightly greased baking sheet with the end of the strip down. Repeat with the remaining strips. Combine the egg yolk and water; brush over the entire pastry. Freeze for 10 minutes. Bake at 425°F for 10 to 12 minutes, or until puffed and golden brown. Remove from the oven and gently slide the molds out of the pastry. Turn off the oven. Return the pastry to the oven for 10 more minutes. Remove from the oven and let cool completely on a wire rack. Combine the whipping cream and powdered sugar; beat at high speed of an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Pipe whipped cream into pastry horns using a decorating bag fitted with a metal tip No. 4B. Sprinkle grated chocolate over the tops of the cream horns. Chill before serving.
